commit.message:
Enable runtime-async unconditionally in System.Private.CoreLib (CoreCLR and NativeAOT) (#126680)

`runtime-async=on` was only set for `Release` builds of CoreCLR's
`System.Private.CoreLib`, and was gated behind a riscv64/loongarch64
exclusion in NativeAOT's `System.Private.CoreLib`. This enables it
unconditionally in both projects.

fix: default appointments view to active statuses

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Split global resource control into scoped controllers

Implement six scoped resource controllers to replace global shared state:

1. Per-peer inbound rate limiting (overlay)
   - TrafficClass enum with 4 classes + SCP exempt
   - PeerRateLimiter with per-class sub-budgets and global aggregate
   - FloodGate global backstop raised to 5000/s

2. Per-peerID connection slot dedup (overlay)
   - PendingConnections with by_address and by_peer_id DashMaps
   - Integrated into all connection establishment paths
   - Stale sweep in tick loop (30s timeout)

3. Dedicated query runtime (app)
   - QueryServer spawned on separate Tokio multi-thread runtime
   - Configured via query.thread_pool_size

4. Meta stream blocking I/O isolation (app)
   - MetaWriter with bounded mpsc channel (cap 16) + dedicated thread
   - Async write for live close, blocking write for catchup
   - Fatal errors set AtomicBool + abort

5. Bounded hot-archive merge (bucket)
   - Replaced HashMap + sort with O(n+m) sorted merge-join
   - O(1) extra memory per entry

6. Seeded validation context at startup (app/herder)
   - seed_validation_context() reads LCL header + Soroban config
   - Called from load_last_known_ledger and bootstrap_from_db
   - Sets soroban_limits and dynamic resource limits immediately

Fix JSON crash in review when toc_buffer has non-JSON at authorities index (#1151)

* Fix JSON parse crash in decode_toc when toc_buffer has non-JSON at authorities index

Old-format toc_buffer rows (pre-Oct 2024, or rows posted by the external tasks
system using the old format) have no authorities column; parts[2] is a genre
string like "article" rather than a JSON array.  All downstream callers
(review helper, _toc partial, update_toc_list) call JSON.parse(parts[2])
without guarding, causing ActionView::Template::Error in production.

Fix: decode_toc now detects non-JSON at parts[2] and inserts an empty
authorities slot, normalising every row to the current 6-column layout at
read time.  A one-time data migration back-fills existing rows so the DB
is also clean.

commit.message:
[improvement](fe) Enhance COALESCE simplification to remove all NullLiterals (#62266)

The existing COALESCE simplification rule in SimplifyConditionalFunction
only removed leading NullLiteral arguments. When a nullable
non-NullLiteral argument was encountered, all remaining arguments
(including trailing/interleaved NullLiterals) were preserved.
Additionally, an early exit prevented any simplification when the first
argument was a nullable non-NullLiteral.

For example, `coalesce(null, null, ..., null, i1, null)` was simplified
to `coalesce(i1, null)` instead of just `i1`.

The improved algorithm:
1. Skips ALL NullLiteral arguments (not just leading ones)
2. Truncates at the first non-nullable argument (subsequent args are
unreachable)
3. Returns the single remaining expression directly when only one is
left

### Release note

Improved COALESCE expression simplification to remove all NULL literal
arguments and truncate unreachable arguments after the first
non-nullable expression.

### Check List (For Author)

- Test: Unit Test
- Behavior changed: No
- Does this need documentation: No

* Fix Kafka CI failures: bypass Aspire TCP proxy with isProxied: false

Root cause: Aspire's TCP proxy (randomly assigned port 35461) doesn't
properly relay Kafka's binary protocol. ApiVersionRequest consistently
times out through the proxy. Additionally, KAFKA_CFG_ADVERTISED_LISTENERS
hardcodes localhost:29094, which doesn't match the proxy's dynamic port.

Fix: Set isProxied: false on the Kafka endpoint so Docker maps port
29094 directly to container port 9094, bypassing the proxy entirely.
This ensures the advertised listener address matches the actual host port.

Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/devstress/My3DLearning/sessions/698b16d3-177d-4c3f-b6b3-e24a19e34316

Co-authored-by: devstress <30769729+devstress@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix Kafka CI: switch from nonexistent bitnami/kafka:3.9.0 to apache/kafka:3.9.0

Root causes:
1. bitnami/kafka:3.9.0 image no longer exists on Docker Hub, so the
   container was never created by Aspire DCP
2. Apache Kafka image requires ://:PORT format (not ://0.0.0.0:PORT)
   in listeners, otherwise it rejects the config at startup
3. isProxied: false with direct port mapping (host:29092 → container:9092)
   is needed because Aspire's ReferenceExpression doesn't resolve the
   proxy port in container env vars

commit.message:
Implement Function.prototype.toString source text preservation

Add byte offset tracking to tokenizer, source span fields to AST function
expression variants, and thread original source text through the compiler
to populate fn_source_texts. This enables Function.prototype.toString to
return the original source text for function expressions and object literal
methods, matching the ECMAScript spec requirement.

- TokenData gains byte_offset field with precomputed byte offset array
- Expr::Function/GeneratorFunction/AsyncFunction/AsyncGeneratorFunction
  gain Option<(usize, usize)> source span field
- Parser computes (start_byte, end_byte) spans for all function/method sites
- Compiler extracts source text via spans and records in fn_source_texts
- VM toString handler already checks fn_source_texts first (no changes needed)

Fixes built-ins/Function/prototype/toString cluster (80/80 pass).
Updates 2 Rust integration tests for new source text behavior.
